paper_title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
passage: CLIP learns by matching images with their associated text and can use text labels for zero-shot recognition.
plain_explanation: CLIP trains on image-caption pairs so matching pictures and descriptions receive compatible representations. At test time, written category names can act as candidate labels, allowing classification without task-specific labeled examples.
rewrite_strategy: Explain training pairs first, then the zero-shot use.
faithfulness_notes: Describes the paper's mechanism; does not claim robust understanding of images or text.
risk_notes: Performance can shift across datasets and inherit biases from web data.
